$comment: European Union column — the Charter of Fundamental Rights of the European Union (legally binding since the Lisbon Treaty, 2009), text retrieved verbatim from EUR-Lex (web). It is the MOST COMPLETE of the four on paper: the only column with ZERO fully-unbacked UDHR articles (25 backed, 5 structural, 0 none), because it is the only instrument that explicitly enumerates socio-economic rights — social security (Art 34), health care (Art 35), and, uniquely among all four columns, rest and leisure / paid annual leave (Art 31(2), backing UDHR 24 that no national constitution here reaches). The essential honest caveat is jurisdictional and lives in meta.remedy: Art 51 confines the Charter to matters within the scope of EU law, so completeness on paper does NOT mean it reaches every national situation. It is also supranational, not a national constitution — different in kind from the UK, German and US columns.

meta.name: European Union

instrument.title: Charter of Fundamental Rights of the European Union

meta.remedy_note: The Court of Justice of the EU (CJEU) can annul EU acts that breach the Charter, and national courts must disapply national law conflicting with it — a strike remedy. But it is SCOPE-LIMITED by Art 51: the Charter binds the EU institutions and the Member States 'only when they are implementing Union law.' It does not reach purely national matters outside the field of EU law, so its strong coverage on paper is bounded by jurisdiction, not by remedy strength. No eternity clause, but the founding Treaties are extremely hard to amend (unanimity + national ratification).
meta.scope_note: Art 51 confines the Charter to the Member States 'only when they are implementing Union law'. For a given national decision the Charter's applicability must first be established (an EU-law nexus), so — unlike a national constitution — it does not reach every in-domain decision.

invariants.udhr: Art 1 — free and equal in dignity
invariants.right: CFR Art 1 — human dignity, inviolable
invariants.text: Human dignity is inviolable. It must be respected and protected.

invariants.right: CFR Art 21 — non-discrimination
CFR Art 2 (life) + Art 6 (liberty and security)
CFR Art 5 — prohibition of slavery and forced labour
CFR Art 4 — prohibition of torture
invariants.text: No one shall be subjected to torture or to inhuman or degrading treatment or punishment.

invariants.right: CFR Art 20 (equality before the law); legal personality implicit
CFR Art 20 (equality before the law) + Art 21
CFR Art 47 — effective remedy before a tribunal (explicit)
invariants.text: Everyone whose rights and freedoms guaranteed by the law of the Union are violated has the right to an effective remedy before a tribunal in compliance with the conditions laid down in this Article.

invariants.right: CFR Art 6 — liberty and security
CFR Art 47 — fair trial

invariants.udhr: Art 11 — presumption of innocence; no retroactive law
invariants.right: CFR Art 48 (presumption of innocence) + Art 49 (legality, no retroactivity)
CFR Art 7 (private and family life) + Art 8 (personal data -- distinctively strong)
invariants.text: Everyone has the right to the protection of personal data concerning him or her.

invariants.right: CFR Art 45 — freedom of movement and residence
invariants.text: Every citizen of the Union has the right to move and reside freely within the territory of the Member States.

invariants.right: CFR Art 18 — right to asylum (explicit)
invariants.text: The right to asylum shall be guaranteed with due respect for the rules of the Geneva Convention of 28 July 1951 and the Protocol of 31 January 1967 relating to the status of refugees and in accordance with the Treaty on European Union and the Treaty on the Functioning of the European Union.

invariants.right: No distinct Charter article; EU citizenship rests in the Treaties (TFEU), not the Charter
CFR Art 9 (right to marry and found a family) + Art 33 (family and professional life)
CFR Art 17 — right to property
CFR Art 10 — freedom of thought, conscience and religion
CFR Art 11 — expression and information
invariants.text: Everyone has the right to freedom of expression. This right shall include freedom to hold opinions and to receive and impart information and ideas without interference by public authority and regardless of frontiers.

invariants.right: CFR Art 12 — assembly and association
CFR Art 39 (European Parliament elections) + Art 40 (municipal) — EU/municipal, not national
CFR Art 34 — social security and social assistance (explicit)
invariants.text: The Union recognises and respects the entitlement to social security benefits and social services providing protection in cases such as maternity, illness, industrial accidents, dependency or old age, and in the case of loss of employment.

invariants.right: CFR Art 15 (work) + Arts 27-28 (info/consultation, collective bargaining) + Art 31 (fair conditions)
CFR Art 31(2) — daily/weekly rest and paid annual leave (the ONLY column here that backs this)
invariants.text: Every worker has the right to limitation of maximum working hours, to daily and weekly rest periods and to an annual period of paid leave.

invariants.right: CFR Art 34(3) (decent existence / housing assistance) + Art 35 (health care)
invariants.text: Everyone has the right of access to preventive health care and the right to benefit from medical treatment under the conditions established by national laws and practices.

invariants.right: CFR Art 14 — right to education
CFR Art 13 (arts and sciences) + Art 22 (cultural diversity) — production/diversity, not a participatory right
CFR Title IV (Solidarity) as the social-order expression; no single article
CFR Art 52 — limitations must be law-based, respect the essence, be proportionate (limits, not duties)
CFR Art 54 — prohibition of abuse of rights (explicit)
invariants.text: Nothing in this Charter shall be interpreted as implying any right to engage in any activity or to perform any act aimed at the destruction of any of the rights and freedoms recognised in this Charter.
